tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who fixed the first foundation,
sm: the one who fixed the first foundation,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.mc: Participial phrase opening the creation catalogue.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: πήξας. Object: τὴν πρώτην βαλβῖδα.
syn.ptc: Circumstantial participle naming an act of cosmic ordering.
syn.rhet: The catalogue begins with foundational cosmic structuring.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who hung the earth,
sm: the one who hung the earth,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: κρεμάσας. Object: τὴν γῆν.
syn.ptc: The earth is pictured as suspended, not self-grounded.
syn.rhet: The cosmic imagery intensifies the act of creation.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who quenched the abyss,
sm: the one who quenched the abyss,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: σβέσας. Object: ἄβυσσον.
syn.ptc: The abyss is treated as something extinguished or subdued.
syn.rhet: Cosmic chaos is subordinated to creative power.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who stretched out the firmament,
sm: the one who stretched out the firmament,
emd: Normalized the trailing OCR quotation mark to comma punctuation.

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: ἐκτείνας. Object: τὸ στερέωμα.
syn.ptc: The firmament is presented as the result of divine extension.
syn.rhet: The creation catalogue moves from abyss to firmament.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who adorned the world,
sm: the one who ordered the world,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: κοσμήσας. Object: τὸν κόσμον.
syn.ptc: κόσμος can mean both ornament and ordered world, fitting the cosmic register.
syn.rhet: Creation is portrayed as adornment as well as order.

src: ὁ τοὺς ἐν οὐρανῷ ἁρμόσας ἀστέρας,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who arranged the stars in heaven,
sm: the one who arranged the stars in heaven,

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: ἁρμόσας. Object: τοὺς ... ἀστέρας. Locative phrase: ἐν οὐρανῷ.
syn.ptc: The stars are treated as a fitted arrangement.
syn.rhet: The orderly heavens are added to the creation panorama.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who brightened the luminaries,
sm: the one who brightened the luminaries,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ and normalized the trailing OCR quotation mark to comma punctuation.

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: λαμπρύνας. Object: τοὺς φωστῆρας.
syn.ptc: The celestial lights are made radiant by the divine act.
syn.rhet: Light itself is placed under the creator's command.

src: ὃ τοὺς ἐν οὐρανῷ ποιήσας ἀγγέλους,"
ana: ὁ τοὺς ἐν οὐρανῷ ποιήσας ἀγγέλους,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who made the angels in heaven,
sm: the one who made the angels in heaven,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ and normalized the trailing OCR quotation mark to comma punctuation.

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: ποιήσας. Object: τοὺς ... ἀγγέλους. Locative phrase: ἐν οὐρανῷ.
syn.ptc: The heavenly angels are included within the created order.
syn.rhet: The creation catalogue now reaches the angelic realm.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who fixed thrones there,
sm: the one who fixed thrones there,
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: πήξας. Object: τοὺς ... θρόνους. Adverb: ἐκεῖ.
syn.ptc: The heavenly thrones are fixed in place by the creator.
syn.rhet: The ordering of heaven is completed by enthroned stability.

src: ὃ τὸν ἐπὶ γῆς ἀναπλασάμενος ἄνθρωπον.
ana: ὁ τὸν ἐπὶ γῆς ἀναπλασάμενος ἄνθρωπον.
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who fashioned man on earth.
sm: the one who fashioned man on earth.
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.mc: Final participial phrase of the creation catalogue.
syn.svo: Implicit referent: God/Christ. Participle: ἀναπλασάμενος. Object: τὸν ... ἄνθρωπον. Locative phrase: ἐπὶ γῆς.
syn.ptc: Humanity is the climactic created object in the list.
syn.rhet: The sequence closes from cosmos back to the human being.

src: οὗτος ἦν ὃ ἐκλεξάμενός σε καὶ καθοδηγήσας σε
ana: οὗτος ἦν ὁ ἐκλεξάμενός σε καὶ καθοδηγήσας σε
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This was the one who chose you and guided you
sm: This was the one who chose you and guided you
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.mc: Main identification clause with participial expansion.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the sentence continues in the following lines with the path of guidance.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Verb: ἦν. Participles: ἐκλεξάμενός, καθοδηγήσας. Object: σε.
syn.rhet: The divine identity is tied to election and guidance of Israel.

src: ἀπὸ τοῦ Ἀδὰμ ἐπὶ τὸν Νῶε,
lit: from Adam to Noah,
sm: from Adam to Noah,

syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; it continues the directional list after καθοδηγήσας.
syn.rhet: Salvation history is traced by succession of names.

src: ἀπὸ τοῦ Νῶε ἐπὶ τὸν Ἀβραάμ,
lit: from Noah to Abraham,
sm: from Noah to Abraham,

syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; the directional series continues.
syn.rhet: The chain advances through the patriarchs.

ana: ἀπὸ τοῦ Ἀβραάμ ἐπὶ τὸν Ἰσὰκ
lit: from Abraham to Isaac
sm: from Abraham to Isaac,

syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; the genealogical sequence continues.
syn.rhet: The ancestral progression remains tightly linear.

src: καὶ τὸν ᾿Ιακὼβ καὶ τοὺς ιβ πατριάρχας.
lit: and to Jacob and the twelve patriarchs.
sm: and to Jacob and the twelve patriarchs.

syn.mc: Final member of the directional genealogy.
syn.svo: No finite verb in this line; it completes the chain begun in line 653.
syn.rhet: The genealogy culminates in Jacob and the twelve patriarchs.

src: οὗτος ἦν ὁ καθοδηγήσας σε εἰς Αἴγυπτον,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This was the one who guided you into Egypt,
sm: This was the one who guided you into Egypt,

syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Verb: ἦν. Participial predicate: ὁ καθοδηγήσας. Object: σε. Destination: εἰς Αἴγυπτον.
syn.rhet: The exodus pattern is folded into the christological subject.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: and having guarded you and there having nourished [you].
sm: and having guarded you and nourished you there.

syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 657. Participles: διαφυλάξας, διαθρεψάμενος. Object: σε.
syn.ptc: Two coordinated participles expand the protective action.
syn.rhet: Guarding and nourishing complete the Egypt motif.

src: οὗτος ἦν ὃ φωταγωγήσας σε ἐν στύλῳ
ana: οὗτος ἦν ὁ φωταγωγήσας σε ἐν στύλῳ
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This was the one who guided you in a pillar of light
sm: This was the one who guided you by a pillar of light
emd: Corrected transmitted ὃ to ὁ; the substantival participle requires the article.

syn.mc: Main identification clause with participial expansion.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 660 continues the coordinated image.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτος. Verb: ἦν. Participial predicate: ὁ φωταγωγήσας. Object: σε. Locative phrase: ἐν στύλῳ.
syn.ptc: The participle is circumstantial, evoking Exodus guidance.
syn.rhet: Light and pillar language evoke the wilderness theophany.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and sheltered you in a cloud,
sm: and sheltered you in a cloud,

syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 659. Participle: σκεπάσας. Object: σε. Locative phrase: ἐν νεφέλῃ.
syn.ptc: The cloud-pillar completes the paired guidance image.
syn.rhet: Fire/light and cloud are paired as divine protection.

ana: ὁ τεμὼν Ἐρυθρὰν καὶ διαγαγών σε
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who split the Red [Sea] and led you through,
sm: the one who split the Red Sea and led you through,
emd: Removed the stray initial breathing mark from ᾿Ερυθράν.

syn.mc: Appositive participial phrase continuing the exodus sequence.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 662 completes the object pair.
syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 659. Participles: τεμὼν, διαγαγών. Object: σε. The adjective Ἐρυθράν is substantival.
syn.ptc: The sea-crossing is cast as a decisive salvific act.
syn.rhet: The Red Sea miracle is compressed into two verbs: split and lead through.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: and having destroyed your enemy.
sm: and having destroyed your enemy.

syn.mc: Final participial member of the exodus sequence.
syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 661. Participle: ἀπολέσας. Object: τὸν ἐχθρόν σου.
syn.ptc: The enemy's destruction completes the deliverance motif.
syn.rhet: Deliverance includes both passage and defeat of the pursuer.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ ἐξ οὐρανοῦ σε μανναδοτήσας,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one who gave you manna from heaven,
sm: This is the one who gave you manna from heaven,

syn.mc: Identification clause introducing the provision sequence.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate participle: ὁ ... μανναδοτήσας. Source phrase: ἐξ οὐρανοῦ.
syn.rhet: The wilderness gifts are now explicitly named.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who gave you drink from the rock,
sm: the one who gave you drink from the rock,

syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 663. Participle: ποτίσας. Object: σε. Source phrase: ἐκ πέτρας.
syn.ptc: The rock-water miracle is evoked with a concise participle.
syn.rhet: Provision continues after manna.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who gave you the law at Horeb,
sm: the one who gave you the law at Horeb,

syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 663. Participle: νομοθετήσας. Indirect object: σοι. Locative phrase: ἐν Χωρήβ.
syn.rhet: The gifts move from sustenance to covenant law.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who allotted the land to you,
sm: the one who allotted the land to you,
emd: Normalized the stray comma-colon punctuation to a comma.

syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 663. Participle: κληροδοτήσας. Indirect object: σοι. Locative phrase: ἐν γῇ.
syn.ptc: The inheritance gift rounds out the land promise.
syn.rhet: Provision extends from wilderness to promised land.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who sent the prophets to you,
sm: the one who sent the prophets to you,

syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 663. Participle: ἐξαποστείλας. Indirect object: σοι. Object: τοὺς προφήτας.
syn.ptc: Prophets are part of the same divine provision.
syn.rhet: The sequence expands from material gifts to prophetic witness.

tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who raised up your kings.
sm: the one who raised up your kings.

syn.mc: Final participial phrase of the provision list.
syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 663. Participle: ἐγείρας. Object: τοὺς βασιλεῖς. Genitive: σου.
syn.ptc: Kings, like prophets, are framed as divine provision.
syn.rhet: The sequence closes with political as well as prophetic leadership.

src: οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ πρός σε ἀφικόμενος,
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: This is the one who came to you,
sm: This is the one who came to you,

syn.mc: Identification clause opening the healing sequence.
syn.svo: Subject: οὗτός. Verb: ἐστιν. Predicate participle: ὁ ... ἀφικόμενος. Directional phrase: πρός σε.
syn.rhet: The figure now appears as the one who finally comes near.

tok: pres act ptcp acc pl m
aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: the one who healed your afflicted ones
sm: the one who healed your afflicted ones

syn.mc: Participial phrase that continues onto the next source line.
syn.sub: No subordinate clause; the sentence is completed in line 671.
syn.svo: Implicit subject continues from line 669. Participle: θεραπεύσας. Object: τοὺς πάσχοντάς σου.
syn.ptc: The healing title begins here and is completed by the following line.
syn.rhet: The passage turns from provision to healing and resurrection.
